verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase anchored exclusively to the inner/cytoplasmic leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation (Gly-2) and palmitoylation (Cys-3). It has no transmembrane domain, no extracellular domain, and no GPI anchor; it operates entirely on the cytoplasmic face. No evidence exists for surface exposure on the outer leaflet. All therapeutic strategies (small-molecule kinase inhibitors) are intracellular. The inner-leaflet lipid anchor means it is wash-resistant on the cytoplasmic face but completely inaccessible from the extracellular space.
